message:
test(harness): stabilize cli_robot introspect test and seed_codex_session filename contract

Two independent but small robustness tweaks to integration-test
scaffolding that kept flaking when run under tighter stack budgets
or different working directories:

1) tests/cli_robot.rs — introspect_commands_match_clap_subcommands

   `Cli::command()` drags the entire clap command tree into the
   caller's stack frame. On the default test thread stack this
   was fine; under `--test-threads=N` with more restrictive
   per-thread budgets we'd occasionally overflow. Other tests in
   this file already wrap expensive clap construction with
   `run_on_large_stack(|| { ... })` (a helper that re-executes
   its closure on a thread with a larger `stack_size`), so the
   only reason this test wasn't similarly wrapped was
   oversight. Wrapped the whole body — JSON fetch, clap walk,
   set-equality assertion — in `run_on_large_stack`. No
   behavioral change; this just stops the intermittent stack
   overflow on constrained runners.

2) tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs — cass_cmd + seed_codex_session

   - cass_cmd(home) now sets `.current_dir(home)` before passing
     the Command back. Without this, cass would inherit the
     test runner's cwd, and the federated publish path (which
     normalizes relative paths against the process cwd) could
     land index files outside the per-test TempDir. Every other
     env variable already points at `home`; this is the one
     extra setter that makes the whole sandbox airtight.
   - seed_codex_session(): the test fixture was writing session
     files whose `id` payload field and on-disk filename did not
     necessarily start with `rollout-`. The codex connector
     (post-commit 82f75552 / ah2x6) treats non-prefixed files as
     legacy artifacts and filters them out before the publish
     crash window. Added an `actual_filename` local that prefixes
     `rollout-` if the caller didn't, and threads it through both
     the session_meta payload's `id` and the `fs::write(sessions.
     join(...))` sink so the fixture always emits a codex-shaped
     filename. Callers that already pass `rollout-...` are
     unchanged.

Both changes are test-only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
